expectations.text: Output identifies this as ANC Contact 1 (first contact, <=12 weeks)
Iron supplementation recommended (either 30-60mg daily OR 60mg weekly intermittent based on population anemia prevalence)
expectations.evidence: Iron: 60mg elemental iron weekly (intermittent supplementation) — Population anemia prevalence is <40% in this setting, and patient is not anemic (Hb 12.1 g/dL). Per WHO guidelines for low anemia-prevalence populations, weekly intermittent iron supplementation is recommended.

expectations.text: Blood group, Rh factor, hemoglobin, HIV, syphilis tests listed as required assessments
expectations.evidence: Assessments section lists: Blood group & Rh: A Rh-positive, Hemoglobin: 12.1 g/dL, HIV test: Negative, Syphilis test (RPR): Non-reactive — all checked off as completed.
Pre-eclampsia prophylaxis risk: LOW — No high-risk factors... Does not meet threshold for moderate risk (requires >=2 moderate risk factors). No aspirin or calcium supplementation indicated.

expectations.text: Next contact scheduled at approximately 20 weeks

expectations.evidence: No Danger Signs section appears in the note (the template specifies it is only included if danger signs are present), and no danger signs are mentioned anywhere in the note.

Disclaimer: This is a decision-support tool based on WHO ANC guidelines (2016 recommendations, DAK). Clinical judgment by a qualified healthcare provider is always required. This tool does not replace clinical assessment.
